newsSince: Genuinely new window news: a June 15 MLCC-sector rally (Taiyo Yuden +23%) and a June 17 Digitimes report that it is boosting AI-server MLCC capacity while resisting some price hikes; Goldman frames MLCCs as the 3rd-largest AI-server cost with a widening structural deficit to 2030. Thesis-supportive but the stock is +361% YTD and gave back ground (-5.8%) over the window.

sources.label: Taiyo Yuden to boost AI-server MLCC capacity (Digitimes)
MLCC concept stocks surge; Taiyo Yuden +23% (TradingKey)

verdicts.rankRationale: Passives-alpha lens: new evidence (June 17 AI-server MLCC capacity build, GS structural-deficit-to-2030 framing) genuinely strengthens the inductor/MLCC bottleneck thesis. But at +361% YTD on a crowding-sensitive 'rerated benchmark' list the residual alpha is thin. Small +1, hold rank.
verdicts.bull: AI-server MLCC sales up 80%+, structural deficit widening to 2030, capacity expansion and price support.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck real but recognized; +361% YTD leaves little residual mispricing.
verdicts.bear: Crowded, flow-dominated benchmark; resisting price hikes caps near-term ASP upside; sharp whipsaws.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI-server MLCC capacity ramp and tightening high-cap supply/demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating chase reversing on any AI-capex pause.
